local_names.chinese_simplified: 可口可乐 / 零度可口可乐
local_names.chinese_traditional: 可口可樂 / 零度可口可樂
local_names.romanized_local: Kě-kǒu Kě-lè / Líng Dù Kě-kǒu Kě-lè

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301638 covers cola drink as a category; no separate FoodOn URI exists for Coca-Cola branded product specifically. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KN covers cola-type soft drinks as a category. No brand-specific FoodEx2 code exists for Coca-Cola or Coca-Cola Zero Sugar. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category. No brand-specific SNOMED CT code exists. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: high-fructose corn syrup or sugar (regular Coke)
aspartame and acesulfame potassium (Coke Zero Sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of Coca-Cola or Coca-Cola Zero Sugar is 330 ml, equivalent to approximately 330 g, as commonly served in Singapore and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14400); Carbonated beverage, cola, with aspartame, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14416)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Regular Coke values sourced from USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400 (carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine). Coke Zero Sugar values sourced from USDA SR Legacy NDB 14416 (carbonated beverage, cola, with aspartame, contains caffeine). This record represents the combined product pair as served in Singapore. Per-100g values reflect regular Coca-Cola as the primary nutritional reference; Coke Zero Sugar macros are effectively zero and documented separately in notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Coca-Cola (Coca-Cola Company, USA)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular Coca-Cola is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Table of GI and GL Values (Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76:5–56). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 31.7) / 100 ≈ 19.9, based on 330 ml serving with 9.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Coke Zero Sugar has a GI of effectively 0 (no glycemic carbohydrates); the GI of 63 applies to regular Coca-Cola only and is the primary reference for this combined record.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; regular Coke contains approximately 4 mg sodium per 100 ml
hypertension.guidance: Regular Coca-Cola and Coca-Cola Zero Sugar are both low in sodium (approximately 4 mg per 100 ml), so neither directly raises blood pressure through sodium load; however, the high sugar content of regular Coke may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of hypertension, and individuals with hypertension should prefer Coke Zero Sugar or limit regular Coke intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content and medium glycemic index (GI 63) in regular Coca-Cola; Coke Zero Sugar contains no glycemic carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Regular Coca-Cola contains approximately 9.6 g of free sugars per 100 ml (GI 63, medium), delivering approximately 31.7 g of sugar per 330 ml can and a glycemic load of approximately 19.9, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; Coca-Cola Zero Sugar contains no sugar or glycemic carbohydrates and is the preferred option, though individuals should be aware of the ongoing debate regarding artificial sweeteners and insulin response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake from regular Coke is associated with elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular Coca-Cola provides approximately 9.6 g of free sugars per 100 ml, and habitual consumption is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk per WHO and AHA guidelines on free sugar intake; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid regular Coke and may choose Coca-Cola Zero Sugar as a lower-risk alternative, while remaining mindful of total dietary sugar and caloric balance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola and Coca-Cola Zero Sugar are globally recognised carbonated soft drinks manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. Regular Coca-Cola is a sweetened cola beverage containing carbonated water, high-fructose corn syrup or sucrose, caramel colour, phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. It delivers approximately 37 kcal per 100 ml and 9.6 g of free sugars per 100 ml, with a glycemic index of 63 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 19.9 per standard 330 ml can. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar is a no-calorie reformulation that replaces sugar with aspartame and acesulfame potassium, providing effectively zero calories, zero carbohydrates, and zero glycemic load. Both variants are carbonated, dark brown in colour, and have a characteristic sweet-caramel flavour with a slightly acidic finish from phosphoric acid and a mild caffeine note. In Singapore, both products are widely available as accompaniments to seafood, crab, zi char, and Chinese restaurant meals, and are commonly listed on menus as 'Coke / Coke Zero'. Clinically, regular Coke is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its free sugar content and medium GI; Coke Zero Sugar is the preferred alternative for glycaemic management. Neither variant contains meaningful protein, fat, dietary fibre, or micronutrients. Both are ultra-processed commercial beverages. The phosphoric acid content may have implications for dental enamel and bone mineral density with excessive long-term consumption.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
Is Coke Zero safe for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of Coca-Cola?
Can I drink Coke if I have Type 2 Diabetes?
Difference between Coke and Coke Zero nutrition
Is Coke Zero suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for carbonated cola beverages (NDB 14400 and NDB 14416), which are exact matches for regular Coca-Cola and diet/zero cola formulations. GI value of 63 for regular Coca-Cola is directly reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Table of GI and GL Values (Foster-Powell et al., 2002). All values are well-established and consistent across multiple approved sources.
notes: This record covers the combined menu item 'Coke / Coke Zero' as listed on field survey data by seafood and zi char restaurants. Per-100g nutritional values reflect regular Coca-Cola (USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400) as the primary reference. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar (USDA SR Legacy NDB 14416) has effectively zero energy, zero protein, zero fat, zero carbohydrate, and zero sugar per 100 ml; these are confirmed zero by product formulation and USDA source data. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14400). fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14400). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — carbonated soft drink contains no dietary fibre by formulation. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — plant-derived beverage contains no cholesterol. All vitamin fields (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero as cola beverages are not a source of vitamins per USDA SR Legacy. iron_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14400. GI of 63 applies to regular Coca-Cola only; Coke Zero Sugar has no glycemic carbohydrates and an effective GI of 0. Glycemic load per serving of 19.9 is calculated for regular Coca-Cola in a 330 ml serving. The cuisine tag 'Crab' in the input reflects the restaurant context (seafood/crab restaurants on field survey) rather than the food's own cuisine classification; this record correctly classifies the beverage as Global/International. Input cuisine_tags (Crab, Malaysian, Zi Char, Chinese, Seafood Dish, Lobster) describe the restaurant context, not the beverage itself.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that GI must be numeric > 0 (regular Coke GI=63 applied). Zero macro justifications added to notes. Sentinel value -1 documentation added for magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg. Ontology match_type values set to 'closest_parent' only (no invented values). aliases_by_language consistency check performed and all entries verified in flat aliases list. Multilingual names added for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi per Singaporean cuisine rules.

Iteration 2: Verified all hard gates and Q criteria. Confirmed aliases_by_language entries all present verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed zero-macro justification phrases present in notes. Confirmed sentinel -1 values documented. Confirmed ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Final decision set to PASS.
